channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Black Hat USA
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Cisco source code stolen in Trivy-linked dev environment breach
item.description: Cisco has suffered a cyberattack after threat actors used stolen credentials from the recent Trivy supply chain attack to breach its internal development environment and steal source code belonging to...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks on Southeast Asian Government Networks
item.description: A high-severity security flaw in the TrueConf client video conferencing software has been exploited in the wild as a zero-day as part of a campaign targeting government entities in Southeast Asia dubb...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Rethinking Vulnerability Management Strategies for Mid-Market Security
item.description: Intruder's Chris Wallis argues mid-market teams should prioritize CVE remediation speed over vulnerability counts, while expanding defenses beyond CVEs to include attack surface management....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AI and Quantum Are Forcing a Rethink of Digital Trust
item.description: In a conversation with Dark Reading’s Terry Sweeney, DigiCert CEO Amit Sinha explains how AI-driven identities and quantum threats are reshaping the foundations of digital trust....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: How to Categorize AI Agents and Prioritize Risk
item.description: AI agent risk isn't equal, it scales with access to systems and level of autonomy. Token Security explains how CISOs should categorize agents and prioritize what to secure first. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers compromise Axios npm package to drop cross-platform malware
item.description: Hackers hijacked the npm account of the Axios package, a JavaScript HTTP client with 100M+ weekly downloads, to deliver remote access trojans to Linux, Windows, and macOS systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Iran Deploys 'Pseudo-Ransomware,' Revives Pay2Key Operations
item.description: Iranian APTs are blurring the lines between state-sponsored and cybercriminal activities to target high-impact US organizations....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Vertex AI Vulnerability Exposes Google Cloud Data and Private Artifacts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a security "blind spot" in Google Cloud's Vertex AI platform that could allow artificial intelligence (AI) agents to be weaponized by an attacker to gain unaut...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft fixes Outlook Classic crashes caused by Teams Meeting add-in
item.description: ​Microsoft has resolved a known issue that rendered the classic Outlook email client unusable for users who enabled the Microsoft Teams Meeting Add-in. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The AI Arms Race – Why Unified Exposure Management Is Becoming a Boardroom Priority
item.description: The cybersecurity landscape is accelerating at an unprecedented rate. What is emerging is not simply a rise in the number of vulnerabilities or tools, but a dramatic increase in speed. Speed of attack...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Silver Fox Expands Asia Cyber Campaign with AtlasCross RAT and Fake Domains
item.description: Chinese-speaking users are the target of an active campaign that uses typosquatted domains impersonating trusted software brands to deliver a previously undocumented remote access trojan named AtlasCr...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hacker charged with stealing $53 million from Uranium crypto exchange
item.description: U.S. prosecutors have charged a Maryland man with stealing more than $53 million after hacking the Uranium Finance crypto exchange twice and laundering the proceeds through a cryptocurrency mixer. [.....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Dutch Finance Ministry takes treasury banking portal offline after breach
item.description: The Dutch Ministry of Finance took some of its systems offline, including the digital portal for treasury banking, while investigating a cyberattack detected two weeks ago. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Citrix flaw by Thursday
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) ordered government agencies to patch their Citrix NetScaler appliances against an actively exploited vulnerability by Thursday. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Axios Supply Chain Attack Pushes Cross-Platform RAT via Compromised npm Account
item.description: The popular HTTP client known as Axios has suffered a supply chain attack after two newly published versions of the npm package introduced a malicious dependency that delivers a trojan capable of targ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Healthcare tech firm CareCloud says hackers stole patient data
item.description: Healthcare IT firm CareCloud has disclosed a data breach incident that exposed sensitive data and caused a network disruption lasting approximately eight hours. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI-Powered 'DeepLoad' Malware Steals Credentials, Evades Detection
item.description: The massive amount of junk code that hides the malware's logic from security scans was almost certainly generated by AI, researchers say....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New RoadK1ll WebSocket implant used to pivot on breached networks
item.description: A newly identified malicious implant named RoadK1ll is enabling threat actors to quietly move from a compromised host to other systems on the network. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI-Driven Code Surge Is Forcing a Rethink of AppSec
item.description: In a conversation with Dark Reading’s Terry Sweeney, Black Duck CEO Jason Schmitt explains how AI is reshaping application security and why it must evolve to keep pace....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Critical Citrix NetScaler memory flaw actively exploited in attacks
item.description: Hackers are exploiting a critical severity vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-3055, in Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway appliances to obtain sensitive data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: F5 BIG-IP Vulnerability Reclassified as RCE, Under Exploitation
item.description: CVE-2025-53521 was initially disclosed in October as a high-severity denial-of-service (DoS) flaw, but new information has revealed the bug is actually much more dangerous....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: OpenAI Patches ChatGPT Data Exfiltration Flaw and Codex GitHub Token Vulnerability
item.description: A previously unknown vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT allowed sensitive conversation data to be exfiltrated without user knowledge or consent, according to new findings from Check Point. "A single mali...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Manufacturing and Healthcare Share Struggles with Passwords
item.description: The two key economic sectors struggle with security for a reason: Many insiders view access management as a roadblock, while attackers see it as a way in....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: DeepLoad Malware Uses ClickFix and WMI Persistence to Steal Browser Credentials
item.description: A new campaign has leveraged the ClickFix social engineering tactic as a way to distribute a previously undocumented malware loader referred to as DeepLoad. "It likely uses AI-assisted obfuscation and...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Storm Brews Over Critical, No-Click Telegram Flaw
item.description: The vulnerability, which is allegedly triggered by a corrupted sticker in the messaging app, received a 9.8 CVSS score, but Telegram denies it exists....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Apple adds macOS Terminal warning to block ClickFix attacks
item.description: Apple has introduced a security feature in macOS Tahoe 26.4 that blocks pasting and executing potentially harmful commands in Terminal and alerts users to possible risks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: How to Evaluate AI SOC Agents: 7 Questions Gartner Says You Should Be Asking
item.description: AI SOC agents can reduce alert fatigue, but most teams fail to measure real outcomes. Prophet Security breaks down Gartner's questions for evaluating AI SOC agents and separating real impact from hype...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Telecom Sleeper Cells, LLM Jailbreaks, Apple Forces U.K. Age Checks and More
item.description: Some weeks are loud. This one was quieter but not in a good way. Long-running operations are finally hitting courtrooms, old attack methods are showing up in new places, and research that stopped bein...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 3 SOC Process Fixes That Unlock Tier 1 Productivity
item.description: What is really slowing Tier 1 down: the threat itself or the process around it? In many SOCs, the biggest delays do not come from the threat alone. They come from fragmented workflows, manual triage s...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
